mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate reduces equity risk premiums by confirming economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 9.0 percent signals labor market normalization, boosting consumer spending expectations and equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and residential construction spending amplify wealth effects and construction sector profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive forward returns in at least 55% of triggered periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating consumer price inflation compress equity multiples by increasing the discount rate and signaling future demand destruction, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite simultaneous rate hikes and inflation acceleration, or the forward return differential between tightening and easing periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings momentum, directly supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth shows no correlation with short-term equity returns, or the signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of positive INDPRO months.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding a monthly level of 130000 thousand jobs confirms robust labor demand, reducing recession fears and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The market prices in job growth as inflationary pressure rather than economic strength, resulting in negative forward returns in over 60% of months where payroll levels exceed 130000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous stagnation in real GDP growth and acceleration in consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary environment that erodes corporate profit margins and equity valuations, depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite concurrent GDP stagnation and inflation spikes, or the interaction effect disappears in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader consumer confidence and durable goods spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth fails to predict short-term equity movements, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of months with positive HOUST readings.
